best.bio_interpretation: K-mer frequency (F1=0.655) is competitive with alignment-based features, indicating that global nucleotide composition already encodes source-relevant genomic signatures without alignment.

dnabert_only.bio_interpretation: Explicit SNP alignment (F1=0.634) outperforms general DNABERT embeddings (F1=0.515). Without Salmonella-specific fine-tuning, DNABERT representations are insufficiently specialised for source attribution on this small dataset.

snp_only.bio_interpretation: SNP features show partial source signal (Macro F1=0.634). Class imbalance or genomic similarity across certain source groups limits consistent classification. Partial overlap between food and food-animal classes is biologically expected given shared supply-chain transmission routes.

hybrid.bio_interpretation: Hybrid features (F1=0.635) are comparable to the best single-mode, suggesting that SNP signal dominates and DNABERT adds marginal information on this dataset. Fine-tuning DNABERT could improve the hybrid gain.

kmer_only.bio_interpretation: K-mer frequency (F1=0.655) is competitive with alignment-based features, indicating that global nucleotide composition already encodes source-relevant genomic signatures without alignment.
